name: Maitreya
shortDescription: Maitreya is the future Buddha, prophesied to appear on Earth in the future, achieve complete enlightenment, and teach the pure dharma. He represents the hope for universal enlightenment and the continuation of the Buddhist teachings.
description: Maitreya is the future Buddha, a bodhisattva who will appear on Earth in the future, achieve complete enlightenment, and teach the pure dharma. According to Buddhist tradition, Maitreya is currently residing in Tushita Heaven, awaiting the opportune time for his descent. He is a central figure in many Buddhist schools, particularly those emphasizing the importance of future hope and the continuation of the Buddhist path. The prophecy of Maitreya's arrival provides solace and inspiration to Buddhists, assuring them that even in times of suffering and decline, the possibility of enlightenment remains. Maitreya's teachings are expected to be more accessible and widespread than those of Gautama Buddha, leading to a golden age of dharma. Iconographically, Maitreya is often depicted seated on a throne, signifying his future role as a teaching Buddha, and sometimes holding a water flask, representing the potential for healing and purification. He is also associated with the concept of universal loving-kindness and compassion, embodying the ideal of a bodhisattva who postpones their own nirvana to help all beings achieve enlightenment. The belief in Maitreya's future arrival encourages Buddhists to cultivate virtuous qualities and practice diligently, in preparation for the time when they can receive his teachings directly. Some Buddhist traditions also emphasize the importance of creating a conducive environment for Maitreya's appearance through ethical conduct and social harmony.

sources.title: The Princeton Dictionary of Buddhism
sources.author: Robert E. Buswell Jr. and Donald S. Lopez Jr.

sources.title: In the Image of Tibet: Tibetan Painting After 1950
sources.author: Clare Harris
